further_reading.title: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy
further_reading.description: Anthropic's own framework for thinking about AI safety and risk — useful context for why safety habits matter at every level.

further_reading.title: How Large Language Models Work
further_reading.description: Anthropic's research page — if you want to go deeper on the mechanics of how these models reason and where their limits come from.

further_reading.title: Anthropic Model Card
further_reading.description: Claude's official model card — includes known limitations, failure modes, and evaluation results. Good reference for the trust calibration framework we covered.

further_reading.title: Building Effective Agents
further_reading.description: Anthropic's guide to agentic systems — written for builders but the first half is excellent context on what makes agents different from assistants and when each is appropriate.

modules.title: OpenCode Modes: Plan, Edit, and Agent
search_topics: agentic AI risk controls enterprise policy

further_reading.title: OpenCode Documentation
further_reading.description: Official OpenCode docs — the modes section covers plan, edit, and agent in detail with current screenshots. Worth bookmarking as your primary reference.

modules.title: Local vs Web Execution
search_topics: EU AI Act data residency enterprise 2024 2025

further_reading.title: Anthropic Privacy Policy
further_reading.description: What Anthropic does and doesn't do with data sent through their API — useful to read alongside your org's data handling policy.

further_reading.title: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications
further_reading.description: The security community's canonical list of risks specific to LLM-based tools — prompt injection, data leakage, and more. Good background for understanding why the data safety habits matter.

further_reading.title: Principle of Least Privilege
further_reading.description: NIST's definition and rationale — the same principle that governs system security applies directly to AI tool permissions.

modules.title: What MCP Is and Why It Matters
search_topics: Model Context Protocol MCP adoption enterprise 2024 2025

further_reading.title: Model Context Protocol — Introduction
further_reading.description: The official MCP site — explains the protocol, why it was created, and how servers are structured. The registry lists all publicly available MCP servers.

further_reading.title: MCP Server Registry
further_reading.description: Browse available MCP connectors — useful for seeing what tools your org could potentially connect.

further_reading.title: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Guide
further_reading.description: Anthropic's guide to writing effective prompts and context files — directly applicable to the guidance file patterns we covered.

further_reading.title: Anthropic Docs: Tool Use
further_reading.description: How Claude handles multiple tools in context — useful background if you want to understand why source attribution and conflict resolution work the way they do.

further_reading.title: OpenCode Extensions Documentation
further_reading.description: How to package and share tools built for OpenCode — next step after building your first helper.

further_reading.title: Anthropic Token Counter Tool
further_reading.description: How to count tokens programmatically — useful if you want to build prompts that reliably stay within budget.

further_reading.title: Anthropic Cookbook
further_reading.description: A collection of practical prompt patterns and workflow templates — good starting point for adapting the frameworks from this module to your own work.

further_reading.title: OpenCode Community
further_reading.description: Where to ask questions, share what you've built, and find others solving similar problems.

further_reading.title: Anthropic Status Page
further_reading.description: If something stops working unexpectedly — check here first before troubleshooting locally.
